children.text: introduction to the 'geeky guy' archetype in Hollywood and TV in comparison to the 'brute-ish' joke archetype, and the ways they also participate in the harassment of women in pop culture

How is this behaviour actually being framed by the narrative though? Often, it is supposed to be seen as their 'right' or something that they are entitled to, for being considered nerds by their peers. Or, it is seen as being ultimately harmless or even endearing.
the 'adorkable' framing of these acts is used by the narrative to obstruct the ways in which they are engaging in toxic and inappropriate behaviours
which kind of sub-archetype Howard fits into -- 'pervert with a heart of gold'
